name: Microsoft/ms-custom-headers
description: Custom response header conformance tests (from CustomHeadersTests): the X-Request-Id to X-Correlation-Id correlation contract, and the CosmosDB-specific continuation-token-size request header. The CosmosDB-specific assertions are warningOnly since non-CosmosDB datastores are expected to ignore that header.

test.name: X-Request-Id sent by the client is echoed back as X-Correlation-Id, not as X-Request-Id
test.description: Sending a client-supplied X-Request-Id must result in the server returning that same value in X-Correlation-Id, while the server's own X-Request-Id response header must remain distinct from the client-supplied value.

operation.description: Search Patient with a client-supplied X-Request-Id header

assert.description: X-Correlation-Id response header must equal the client-supplied X-Request-Id value
Server's own X-Request-Id response header must not contain the client-supplied value

test.name: requests without a client X-Request-Id still receive a server-generated X-Request-Id
test.description: Without a client-supplied X-Request-Id, the server must still return HTTP 200 with a non-empty, server-generated X-Request-Id header. The absent-X-Correlation-Id check from the source test is skipped since that outcome depends on whether a reverse proxy is present.

operation.description: Search Patient without any client-supplied request id header

assert.description: X-Request-Id response header must be present and non-empty

test.name: a valid CosmosDB continuation token size limit header does not break the request
test.description: A valid, in-range CosmosDB continuation-token-size header (1-3 Kb) must not cause a failure on any backend.

operation.description: Search Patient with a valid CosmosDB continuation token size limit header

assert.description: Request must succeed with HTTP 200 regardless of backing datastore

test.name: an invalid CosmosDB continuation token size limit header is rejected by CosmosDB-backed servers
test.description: CosmosDB-backed servers must return HTTP 400 for a malformed ('invalid') or out-of-range ('5') continuation-token-size value; warningOnly since other datastores are expected to ignore the header.

operation.description: Search Patient with a malformed (non-numeric) continuation token size limit header

assert.description: CosmosDB-backed servers must return HTTP 400 for a malformed token size (warningOnly: other datastores ignore this header)

operation.description: Search Patient with an out-of-range (too large) continuation token size limit header

assert.description: CosmosDB-backed servers must return HTTP 400 for an out-of-range token size (warningOnly: other datastores ignore this header)
